evidence.quote: we're designing a new building and we don't need the whole thing designed to construction members in CAD, we need to create a set of renders that make the experience of the space vivid

observations.why: extended architecture-render AS-comparison doing argumentative work about MVP fidelity

evidence.quote: Teams integration gates Escher, our actual paying client

observations.why: opens a matched X-gates-Y series repeated across src-17/18/19

evidence.quote: Zoom gates Taylor testing, our most active beta tester

observations.why: second clause in the repeated 'X gates Y' structure begun at src-16

evidence.quote: Google meet gates our org's secondary transcription path and the last org suite surface.

observations.why: third clause continuing the anaphoric 'gates' pattern

evidence.quote: Otter and other transcription tools gate individual developer/entrepreneur testing/adoption

observations.why: fourth and closing clause of the matched 'gates' series, completing a rule-of-four grid

evidence.quote: Simplify.

observations.why: single-word imperative fragment functioning as terse emphasis

evidence.quote: it's not an experiment at the existing MVP product. This is the MVP product, this is the front end that was never built.

observations.why: negated frame set against the asserted frame in deliberate contrast

evidence.quote: the live surface is not a second product or an experiment — it is the MVP product's front end that was never built

observations.why: explicit not-X-but-Y contrast structuring the headline settlement

evidence.quote: I'd say, arguably, no, right?

observations.why: question posed with answer already supplied by the asker via 'arguably, no'

evidence.quote: What we're doing is not providing a home for conversation with AI. We're providing a home for perspective and the integration feed

observations.why: not-X-but-Y matched clause pair defining the product by negation and restatement

evidence.quote: We're always expecting primary source quotes from a specific person, either with interspersed commentary or just a raw transcript.

observations.why: recurring org formula 'primary source' names a settled category rather than describing this instance alone

evidence.quote: we're also arguing that the entire value of the product is that it doesn't do summary until read time, right? So then why are we doing all this filter

observations.why: posed question exposing contradiction between stated principle and practice, answer implied

evidence.quote: at first resolution, every single quote from the call, no summary, no filter

observations.why: matched no-summary/no-filter clause pair defining the layer

evidence.quote: At second resolution, only positions — each expandable to the expressions that support it, footnotes beneath.

observations.why: continues the anaphoric 'At Nth resolution' structure begun in src-79

evidence.quote: Then a narrative layer where positions are the footnotes, and strata above.

observations.why: third beat of the resolution-layer triad, completing a rule-of-three ladder

evidence.quote: an icon that steps an expression up a layer, collapsing siblings under their position

observations.why: spatial 'steps up' / 'collapsing' language treats the data structure as physical terrain

evidence.quote: Not included in the product itself. But we can include them in a one-off produced and brand-styled artifact

observations.why: not-in-product-but-in-artifact contrast structuring the demo/product boundary

evidence.quote: and in there we can show the flocking cause it's fun

observations.why: quoted 'flocking' names the visualization AS bird-flock behavior

evidence.quote: This product changes a lot if we can get extraction costs down enough. If we can get extraction costs down enough that you can actually converse with the thing

observations.why: repeated conditional 'if we can get extraction costs down enough' clause opening consecutive sentences

evidence.quote: what do you receive? And what do you ever see? How much is it going to be?

observations.why: three successive posed questions in a rapid triad probing the same access gap

evidence.quote: this is the real question — that should be okay, right, because they are not actually showing the source?

observations.why: question posed with the answer presumed via 'right' tag and self-answered in the same breath

evidence.quote: annotations that if removed would not move the chat bubbles

observations.why: physical-displacement metaphor testing whether annotation is load-bearing vs decorative

evidence.quote: Theatre must feel like a chat flow with annotations that if removed would not move the chat bubbles or leave empty space.

observations.why: annotations treated AS physical objects that could 'move' bubbles or 'leave empty space'

evidence.quote: Gaps between messages, doesn't read like a conversation.

observations.why: spatial 'gaps' figure for pacing/design failure

evidence.quote: Big bags between messages, does read like a conversation

observations.why: mirrors and inverts src-57's judgment, forming a paired contrast across the two notes

mints.name: RendersNotBlueprints
mints.title: renders, not construction-member CAD
mints.theme: Fidelity is argued through an extended building-design comparison: vivid partial renders stand in contrast to fully engineered blueprints
